design_philosophy.core_loop: Select from multiple G.I. Joe characters, traverse themed stages with branching routes, defeat bosses, rescue operatives, unlock new characters and abilities
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Non-linear stage progression with character-specific unlock requirements creates puzzle-like route dependencies; player must plan character selection and stage order strategically
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command elite G.I. Joe operatives across diverse military missions, leveraging character-specific abilities (jetpacks, swimming, vehicles) to overcome environmental and combat obstacles
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately non-linear progression system where stage accessibility depends on rescued characters and obtained items—rejects linear level progression in favor of player agency and puzzle-solving around availability. Password system complexity suggests developers valued replayability and sequence-breaking exploration over accessibility

difficulty.lives_system: Traditional lives system implied by action game conventions of era
difficulty.continues_system: Password/continue system present, notably complex ('doozy') requiring graph paper
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing with password continuation between sessions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection and ability unlocks provide indirect difficulty scaling; players can sequence stages to obtain power-ups before attempting harder routes

progression.upgrade_system: Character-based progression with weapon/ability unlocks; unused sprites suggest planned swimming and jetpack mechanics tied to specific characters
progression.unlock_mechanism: Rescue specific characters to unlock stages; obtain items to enable new routes. Non-linear gating prevents revisiting stages without memory hacking, enforcing commitment to chosen path
replayability_hooks: Multiple character roster with distinct abilities
Password system enabling selective progression saves

world_building_techniques: Opening/ending cinematics (unused/glitched audio suggests cinematic ambition)
Character rescue missions as plot beats (Duke rescue sequence)
Stage themes tied to military objectives and locations (Snowy Mountain, Atlantis rising)

boss_design.philosophy: Character-specific challenges with distinct attack patterns; Cesspool features sword-based leap-and-swipe mechanics suggesting dramatic martial encounters

faction_indicators: G.I. Joe operatives (player characters: General Hawk, Wet Suit, Road Block, Spirit, Duke)
COBRA forces (minibosses, flying enemies, ground units)
danger_signals: Air-based COBRA attacks from flying vehicles (Mamba, Sea Ray helicopters)
Miniboss archetypes: bouncing melee fighters, burst-fire kneeling gunners, airborne sweepers
Indoor turret defenses and explosive canisters
Inaccessible stages signaling zones under COBRA control until allies rescued
environmental_storytelling: Vehicle sprites (Sea Ray, Mamba, presumably player vehicles) indicate diverse military terrain
Secret statues (chime sound cue) suggest hidden objectives or lore collectibles

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - G.I. Joe: The Atlantis Factor
